title: Gas Money - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Transportation > Gas Money 13 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BEND UNITED METHODIST CHURCH OPEN DOOR MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides access to temporary mail services, a clothing closet, and gasoline cards. 680 NW Bond Street Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Mail room: Monday-Friday 1pm-3pm Clothing closet: Monday/Wednesday 1pm-3pm Compassionate Care Financial Aid: Wednesday 1pm until capacity is reached Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH OF ALBANY INC BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes, clothing, shoes, bedding, personal and feminine hygiene products, diapers, baby food and formula, assistance in acquiring identification documents, gasoline and propane vouchers, and local bus tickets. Availability is subject to donations and eligibility. 1035 SE 2nd Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Food and clothing: By appointment only All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 928-4460 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH OF ROSEBURG EMERGENCY GAS VOUCHER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency gas vouchers and local bus tickets as funding is allowed and assistance is not guaranteed. Program is only accessible once. Vouchers must be used the same day that they are issued. 405 Jerry's Drive Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older with a demonstrated emergency need Hours: Voicemail available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week. Calls will be returned the next business day. Email (541) 672-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HARNEY COUNTY HARNEY HUB SENIOR AND COMMUNITY CENTER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides utility assistance for gas, electric, and heating fuel bills; motel vouchers; one-time gas vouchers; transportation passes; food pantry (visit twice per month); Fresh Alliance program (provides fresh produce, dairy, and bread products); weatherization services; and blood pressure screenings. Offers snack kits. Assistive medical equipment and mobility aids also available. 17 S Alder Street Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: -Food pantry: Households at or below 300% federal poverty level. No proof of income is required. -Fresh Alliance: Unrestricted -Snack kits for those looking to keep moving through the community -Motel vouchers: Homeless individuals requiring one night's stay -Gas vouchers: Must be stuck and looking to continue to move through the community -Transportation passes: Must have no ties to the community and looking to continue to move through the community -Energy Assistance: Household must meet federal poverty guidelines Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm Fresh Alliance: Monday 10am-10:30am Food pantry: Wednesday 10am-1pm, Thursday 4pm-6pm Email (541) 573-6024 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SALVATION ARMY LEBANON EXTENSION BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides electric, water, propane, and rent payment assistance. Also provides gas and bus vouchers to get to medical appointments. 3000 S Santiam Highway Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday 10am-2pm. Last appointments seen at 1:30pm. Email (541) 409-5929 Get Directions More Details SILVERTON AREA COMMUNITY AID TRANSPORTATION AND PRESCRIPTION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance for transportation costs including gasoline vouchers and bus tickets. Also assists with prescription medications (with some exceptions). Gas vouchers must be used at specified location and prescriptions must be filled at one of two participating local pharmacies. 1030 N 1st Street Suite A Silverton, OR 97381 Eligibility: Households must meet Silverton Area Community Aid Income Guidelines Hours: Monday 11am-2pm, Tuesday 3pm-7pm, Thursday 9am-noon Call to schedule an appointment outside of walk-in hours Email (503) 873-3446 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SIUSLAW OUTREACH SERVICES SIUSLAW AREA RESOURCE CENTER COMMUNITY ASSISTANCE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a variety of services including assistance with mortgage, electric, water and heating bills, prescriptions, shower and laundry vouchers, and transportation passes. Provides sleeping bags, tents, tarps, hats, gloves, and hand warmers during the winter season. All services are dependent on available funds. 1536 W 12th Street Florence, OR 97439 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-3:30pm, Friday 10am-4pm Email (541) 997-2816 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH LINCOLN RESOURCES VOUCHER ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with electric, heating fuel, and water bills. Also assists with rent, gas money, and prescriptions. 3710 Crestline Drive Waldport, OR 97394 Eligibility: Unrestricted. Individuals requesting assistance must be full-time residents. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 563-3710 4 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST MARY'S CATHOLIC CHURCH ST MARY'S CATHOLIC CHURCH POOR FUND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency financial assistance for rent payment assistance, electric, natural gas bills, and gas vouchers. Funding is limited. If needs exceed available funding, St. Mary's Poor Fund volunteers will assist in finding other agencies that may be able to cover the remaining expense. 501 NW 25th Street Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Friday 10am-11:30am Email (541) 757-1988 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL LA PINE SOCIAL SERVICES OFFICE SOCIAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency food boxes, clothing for adults, household items (kitchenware and small appliances), prescription assistance, gas money (for job interviews or appointments), emergency motel vouchers, and ID assistance. 51484 Morson Road La Pine, OR 97739 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-noon and 12:30pm-2pm Email (541) 536-6135 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SWEET HOME EMERGENCY MINISTRIES FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides up to $100 of financial assistance for past due water, electricity, and rent bills. Also provides assistance with prescription medications excluding those that are pain medications or mood-altering, gasoline vouchers for verified out of town doctor's appointments only, and propane vouchers up to $25 that can be used at the Sweet Home RV Center. Individuals may only receive assistance once during a 12 month period. 1115 Long Street Sweet Home, OR 97386 Eligibility: Households with income at or below 185% of the FPL. Those seeking assistance with electricity, natural gas or water must have a shut-off notice. Hours: Tuesday/Wednesday/Saturday 9am-11:30am, closed 2nd Saturday of the month Email (541) 367-6504 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TILLAMOOK SERENITY CLUB COMMUNITY RESOURCE ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers financial assistance in the form of gas or grocery gift cards. Limited gift cards are available on a first-come, first-served basis. Monthly bus passes for The Wave Tillamook may be available for people without other forms of transportation. 5012 3rd Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Must be participating in 12-step recovery Hours: 2nd Tuesday of the month Email (503) 842-1115 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TURNING POINT COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER TURNING POINT COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER/FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Hosts a food pantry that also provides rental assistance for eviction prevention when funding is available, utility assistance, rental deposit assistance, prescription expense assistance, clothing, gas vouchers for medical appointments, and a thrift store. Offers application assistance for OHP,SNAP,TANF, LIHEAP, and Community Action Team (CAT) Housing Solutions. Access to faxing, scanning, emailing, and printing services. 220 E Columbia River Highway Clatskanie, OR 97016 Eligibility: Must meet USDA income guidelines for food pantry program Hours: Client Services: Monday 10am-12pm, Tuesday/Thursday 10am-4pm Food Pantry: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 11am-2:30pm Thrift Store: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 10am-4pm Email (503) 728-3126 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BEND UNITED METHODIST CHURCH OPEN DOOR MINISTRIES FISH OF ALBANY INC BASIC NEEDS FISH OF ROSEBURG EMERGENCY GAS VOUCHER PROGRAM HARNEY COUNTY HARNEY HUB SENIOR AND COMMUNITY CENTER SERVICES SALVATION ARMY LEBANON EXTENSION BASIC NEEDS SILVERTON AREA COMMUNITY AID TRANSPORTATION AND PRESCRIPTION ASSISTANCE SIUSLAW OUTREACH SERVICES COMMUNITY ASSISTANCE SERVICES SOUTH LINCOLN RESOURCES VOUCHER ASSISTANCE PROGRAM ST MARY'S CATHOLIC CHURCH ST MARY'S CATHOLIC CHURCH POOR FUND ST VINCENT DE PAUL LA PINE SOCIAL SERVICES OFFICE SOCIAL SERVICES SWEET HOME EMERGENCY MINISTRIES FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE TILLAMOOK SERENITY CLUB COMMUNITY RESOURCE ASSISTANCE TURNING POINT COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER TURNING POINT COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER/FOOD PANTRY
